evals.prompt: I have a crackme binary at /root/gdb-test/libdebug/test/binaries/amd64/brute_test that checks a password character by character. Write a libdebug script to brute-force the correct password by counting breakpoint hits at the comparison function. Save the script to outputs/brute.py
evals.expected_output: A Python script using libdebug that brute-forces the crackme by setting breakpoints at the comparison function and counting hits per character candidate

evals.prompt: Write a libdebug script to trace all syscalls made by /root/gdb-test/libdebug/test/binaries/amd64/handle_syscall_test. Log each syscall name/number and its arguments. Save the script to outputs/trace.py
evals.expected_output: A Python script using libdebug that traces and logs all syscalls with their arguments

evals.prompt: Write a libdebug script that attaches to /root/gdb-test/libdebug/test/binaries/amd64/basic_test_pie, sets a breakpoint at the entry point, steps through the first 10 instructions logging RIP and RAX at each step, then prints the memory map and backtrace. Save the script to outputs/inspect.py
evals.expected_output: A Python script using libdebug that sets a breakpoint at entry, single-steps 10 instructions logging registers, then prints maps and backtrace
